symptoms.symptom_name: Acute metamorphopsia (straight lines appear bent and wavy) in the right eye

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: New metamorphopsia is the classic symptom of macular subretinal fluid and the trigger for urgent referral.
Detachment typically presents with a field defect/curtain and floaters rather than isolated central distortion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dense central scotoma (dark patch in the centre of vision)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Central macular pathology produces a central scotoma with preserved peripheral vision.
A macula-off or large detachment usually causes peripheral field loss; intact periphery favours a focal macular process.
Macular disease spares the peripheral retina.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of eye pain, headache, nausea or haloes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Angle-closure is characteristically painful with haloes, headache and nausea; their absence makes it unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of flashes, new floaters, or a descending curtain/shadow

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Photopsia, a shower of floaters and a curtain are the hallmark symptoms of detachment; their absence lowers its probability but cannot exclude it without examination.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Amaurosis fugax / transient monocular visual loss (TIA)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Amaurosis fugax is transient and resolves within minutes; persistent distortion argues against it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known pre-existing dry AMD with slowly progressive central vision loss and poor low-light adaptation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Established dry AMD is the substrate from which neovascular conversion arises; it is the key risk factor.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive Amsler-grid distortion and central scotoma on bedside testing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-administered Amsler testing objectifies the metamorphopsia and scotoma — cannot be validly performed or verified over telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound drop in central visual acuity in the right eye versus baseline

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Formal visual-acuity testing quantifies the loss; requires an in-person chart and cannot be done via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal external eye and pupils — no afferent pupillary defect, clear cornea, no injection

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clear, white, uninflamed eye with reactive pupils argues against angle-closure; requires clinician examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Drusen in the macular region on direct fundoscopy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Drusen confirm the AMD substrate; fundoscopy is a clinician skill unavailable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Subretinal fluid on optical coherence tomography (OCT)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Optical coherence tomography (OCT) of the macula
investigation_details.expected_result: Subretinal / intraretinal fluid confirming active neovascular AMD
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without OCT the leak cannot be confirmed and anti-VEGF therapy cannot be planned; the diagnosis remains presumptive.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: OCT demonstration of subretinal fluid is the confirmatory finding; only available at the eye clinic, never in telehealth in real time.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vital signs (BP mildly elevated, otherwise within normal limits)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Vitals are not diagnostic for AMD; recorded by the in-person clinician and not obtainable via chat without a home device.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent ophthalmology / rapid-access eye clinic assessment with dilated fundoscopy
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: In-person examination is required to assess the macula and exclude retinal detachment and angle-closure.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Optical coherence tomography (OCT) of the macula
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms subretinal fluid and guides intravitreal anti-VEGF therapy within the treatment window.
